required_response_schema.page_rationale: non-empty page-level rationale tied to rendered and extracted evidence

preset_type_compatibility.text: broad review stratum; section_subtitle may be clean-compatible when text, bbox, and visual role are accurate

status_consistency.clean: requires every candidate finding status to be clean
status_consistency.defect: requires at least one defect candidate finding
status_consistency.substrate_blocked: requires at least one substrate_blocked candidate finding and allows only clean or substrate_blocked findings

suggested_fix_surface.clean: must be null, empty string, or 'none'
suggested_fix_surface.defect: must identify the concrete fix surface

content.text:
You are reviewing PDF Oxide extraction evidence for one page. Compare the original rendered page, the annotated candidate image, the extracted JSON, and the candidate preset questions. Return JSON only. You may classify candidates as clean, defect, unsure, or substrate_blocked. Do not claim that a bug is fixed, patched, resolved, committed, or closed.

Candidate ID discipline:
- candidate_findings[].candidate_id MUST be copied verbatim from the required_candidate_ids list below.
- Return exactly one finding for each required_candidate_ids entry and no other candidate_id values.
- Do not infer, rewrite, or repair candidate_id suffixes from preset_type, extracted JSON type, visual class, or rationale.
- If the visual/extracted evidence suggests a different semantic type, describe that only in status/rationale/suggested_fix_surface; keep candidate_id unchanged.

Preset type compatibility:
- preset_type text is a broad review stratum, not an expected exact semantic type.
- A more specific extracted subtype such as section_subtitle is clean-compatible with preset_type text when its text, bbox, and visual role are accurate.
- Do not report a defect solely because the extracted subtype is more specific than preset_type text.

Suggested fix surface discipline:
- For status clean, suggested_fix_surface MUST be null, an empty string, or the literal string "none".
- For status defect, suggested_fix_surface MUST identify the concrete fix surface, such as pdf_oxide_core, nist_preset, export/schema, UI, or external harness.
- Do not include optional improvement advice in suggested_fix_surface for a clean finding; put clean rationale in rationale only.

Page/candidate status consistency:
- page_status clean requires every candidate finding status to be clean.
- page_status defect requires at least one defect candidate finding.
- page_status substrate_blocked requires at least one substrate_blocked candidate finding and allows only clean or substrate_blocked candidate findings.

    },
    {
      "bbox": [

        0.31880975860396243,

      ],
      "font_name": "TT2",
      "font_size": 16.020000457763672,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:5",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Title",
      "text": "INTRODUCTION",
      "type": "section_heading"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14705882352941177,
        0.15823163889875316,
        0.7191140667285795,
        0.1751593580149641
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 10.020000457763672,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:6",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Body",
      "text": "THE NEED TO PROTECT INFORMATION, SYSTEMS, ORGANIZATIONS, AND INDIVIDUALS",
      "type": "section_subtitle"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14697116029028798,
        0.18873045179578993,
        0.8469282162734886,
        0.30902521538011957
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 10.61999797821045,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:7",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Body",
      "text": "Modern information systems1 can include a variety of computing platforms (e.g., industrial control systems, general purpose computing systems, cyber-physical systems, super computers, weapons systems, communications systems, environmental control systems, medical devices, embedded devices, sensors, and mobile devices such as smart phones and tablets). These platforms all share a common foundation\u2014computers with complex hardware, software and firmware providing a capability that supports the essential mission and business functions of organizations.2",
      "type": "paragraph_block"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14703897713056577,
        0.32259496053059894,
        0.845089781518076,
        0.4937227422540838
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 10.979998588562012,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:8",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Body",
      "text": "Security controls are the safeguards or countermeasures employed within a system or an organization to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the system and its information and to manage information security3 risk. Privacy controls are the administrative, technical, and physical safeguards employed within a system or an organization to manage privacy risks and to ensure compliance with applicable privacy requirements.4 Security and privacy controls are selected and implemented to satisfy security and privacy requirements levied on a system or organization. Security and privacy requirements are derived from applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and mission needs to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information processed, stored, or transmitted and to manage risks to individual privacy.",
      "type": "paragraph_block"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.1470541361889808,
        0.5072910809757734,
        0.8428934134689032,
        0.5766791526717369
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 10.539998054504395,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:9",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Body",
      "text": "The selection, design, and implementation of security and privacy controls5 are important tasks that have significant implications for the operations6 and assets of organizations as well as the welfare of individuals and the Nation. Organizations should answer several key questions when addressing information security and privacy controls:",
      "type": "paragraph_block"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14708595026552287,
        0.583430781508937,
        0.8394090241076899,
        0.686382178104285
      ],
      "font_name": "C2_0",
      "font_size": 10.979998588562012,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:10",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "List",
      "text": "\u2022 What security and privacy controls are needed to satisfy security and privacy requirements and to adequately manage mission/business risks or risks to individuals? \u2022 Have the selected controls been implemented or is there a plan in place to do so? \u2022 What is the required level of assurance (i.e., grounds for confidence) that the selected controls, as designed and implemented, are effective?7",
      "type": "list"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14705882352941177,
        0.7266742436572758,
        0.7791440377827563,
        0.7557423909505209
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 8.5,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:11",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Footnote",
      "text": "1 An information system is a discrete set of information resources organized for the collection, processing, maintenance, use, sharing, dissemination, or disposition of information [OMB A-130].",
      "type": "footnote"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14705882352941177,
        0.7569015194671322,
        0.8381487279156454,
        0.7859696667603772
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 8.571428298950195,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:13",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Footnote",
      "text": "2 The term organization describes an entity of any size, complexity, or positioning within an organizational structure (e.g., a federal agency or, as appropriate, any of its operational elements).",
      "type": "footnote"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14705882352941177,
        0.7872045497701625,
        0.6981442021388634,
        0.8024090853604403
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 8.769230842590332,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:15",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Footnote",
      "text": "3 The two terms information security and security are used synonymously in this publication.",
      "type": "footnote"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14705882352941177,
        0.8035682138770518,
        0.45684804480060254,
        0.8187727494673296
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 8.75,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:16",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Footnote",
      "text": "4 [OMB A-130] defines security and privacy controls.",
      "type": "footnote"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14704900006063623,
        0.8200075554125237,
        0.8403667375153187,
        0.8490757027057686
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 8.399999618530273,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:17",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Footnote",
      "text": "5 Controls provide safeguards and countermeasures in systems security and privacy engineering processes to reduce risk during the system development life cycle.",
      "type": "footnote"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14705882352941177,
        0.8502348312223801,
        0.6120427449544271,
        0.8654393668126579
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 8.399999618530273,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:19",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Footnote",
      "text": "6 Organizational operations include mission, functions, image, and reputation.",
      "type": "footnote"
    },
    {
      "bbox": [
        0.14705882352941177,
        0.8666742498224432,
        0.8278501922009038,
        0.9096060088186553
      ],
      "font_name": "TT0",
      "font_size": 8.0,
      "id": "actual:p28:block:20",
      "page": 28,
      "pdf_page_index": 27,
      "source_type": "Footnote",
      "text": "7 Security and privacy control effectiveness addresses the extent to which the controls are implemented correctly, operating as intended, and producing the desired outcome with respect to meeting the designated security and privacy requirements [SP 800-53A].",
